---
title: "Shadow Tealbook A"
subtitle: "Current Economic and Financial Conditions"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import os, sys
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# ── Paths ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path('.').resolve().parent))
from data.fred_pulls import pull_series, get_series

BLUE   = '#003087'
RED    = '#C41E3A'
GRAY   = '#6B6B6B'
GREEN  = '#2E7D32'
ORANGE = '#E65100'
NAVY2  = '#1565C0'

START  = '2015-01-01'
now    = datetime.today().strftime('%B %d, %Y')

# ── LLM narrative helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
import json, urllib.request

def call_claude(system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str,
                max_tokens: int = 500) -> str:
    """
    Call Claude API to generate a Tealbook-style narrative paragraph.
    Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in environment.
    Falls back to a placeholder if the key is missing.
    """
    api_key = os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', '')
    if not api_key:
        return (
            "_[LLM narrative unavailable — set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to enable "
            "auto-generated interpretive text.]_"
        )
    payload = json.dumps({
        "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
        "system": system_prompt,
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}]
    }).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        data=payload,
        headers={
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "x-api-key": api_key,
            "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01"
        }
    )
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
            result = json.loads(resp.read())
            return result["content"][0]["text"].strip()
    except Exception as e:
        return f"_[LLM call failed: {e}]_"


TEALBOOK_SYSTEM = """You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing a section 
of the Tealbook A — the internal briefing document prepared for FOMC meetings. 
Write 2–3 short declarative paragraphs in the Tealbook style: 
- Third person, no first-person pronouns
- Data-anchored: every claim references a specific number or change
- Present tense for current conditions, forward-looking language draws on provided projections
- No hedging phrases like "it seems" or "may suggest"
- Concise — each paragraph 2–4 sentences
- Do not invent numbers; only use what is given in the user prompt
Respond with the narrative text only, no headers, no markdown formatting."""
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Prepared `{python} now`** — Data sourced from FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis), BLS, and BEA. This shadow Tealbook A is a research project of Purdue University and does not represent the views of the Federal Reserve System.
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 📊 Overview

```{python}
#| label: overview-data
#| include: false

# Pull key snapshot series
gdp_g    = pull_series('A191RL1Q225SBEA', start=START).dropna()
unrate   = pull_series('UNRATE',          start=START).dropna()
core_pce = pull_series('PCEPILFE',        start=START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
payrolls = pull_series('PAYEMS',          start=START).dropna().diff().dropna()
nrou     = pull_series('NROU',            start=START).dropna().resample('MS').interpolate()
t10      = pull_series('DGS10',           start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
t2       = pull_series('DGS2',            start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()

# Latest readings
gdp_latest    = gdp_g.iloc[-1]
gdp_prev      = gdp_g.iloc[-2]
unrate_latest = unrate.iloc[-1]
nrou_latest   = nrou.reindex(unrate.index, method='ffill').iloc[-1]
ugap          = unrate_latest - nrou_latest
core_latest   = core_pce.iloc[-1]
payroll_3m    = payrolls.iloc[-3:].mean()
t10_latest    = t10.iloc[-1]
spread_10_2   = (t10 - t2).iloc[-1]

# SPF proxies (hard-coded from latest Philadelphia Fed release — update each cycle)
spf_gdp_next  = 2.1   # SPF median real GDP next 4 quarters
spf_pce_next  = 2.3   # SPF median PCE inflation next year
fomc_lr_gdp   = 1.8   # FOMC longer-run real GDP SEP central tendency
fomc_lr_unemp = 4.1   # FOMC longer-run unemployment SEP
fomc_lr_pce   = 2.0   # FOMC longer-run PCE inflation target
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-scoreboard

from IPython.display import display, HTML

rows = [
    ("Real GDP Growth (latest quarter, SAAR)", f"{gdp_latest:.1f}%",
     "▲" if gdp_latest > gdp_prev else "▼"),
    ("Unemployment Rate",                       f"{unrate_latest:.1f}%",
     f"Gap vs. natural rate: {ugap:+.1f} pp"),
    ("Core PCE Inflation (YoY)",                f"{core_latest:.1f}%",
     f"Target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%"),
    ("Avg Monthly Payroll Gain (3-mo)",         f"{payroll_3m:,.0f}K", ""),
    ("10-yr Treasury Yield",                    f"{t10_latest:.2f}%",
     f"10s–2s spread: {spread_10_2:+.2f} pp"),
]

html = """
<table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.92rem;
              font-family:'Source Sans Pro',sans-serif;">
  <thead>
    <tr style="background:#003087;color:white;">
      <th style="padding:8px 12px;text-align:left;">Indicator</th>
      <th style="padding:8px 12px;text-align:right;">Latest</th>
      <th style="padding:8px 12px;text-align:left;">Note</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
"""
for i, (ind, val, note) in enumerate(rows):
    bg = "#f5f7fb" if i % 2 == 0 else "white"
    html += f"""
    <tr style="background:{bg};">
      <td style="padding:7px 12px;">{ind}</td>
      <td style="padding:7px 12px;text-align:right;font-weight:600;">{val}</td>
      <td style="padding:7px 12px;color:#555;">{note}</td>
    </tr>"""

html += """
  </tbody>
  <tfoot>
    <tr style="background:#f0f0f0;font-size:0.78rem;color:#777;">
      <td colspan="3" style="padding:6px 12px;">
        SPF projections: GDP {spf}% | PCE {pce}% &nbsp;·&nbsp;
        FOMC longer-run: GDP {lr_gdp}% | Unemployment {lr_u}% | PCE {lr_pce}%
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tfoot>
</table>
""".format(
    spf=spf_gdp_next, pce=spf_pce_next,
    lr_gdp=fomc_lr_gdp, lr_u=fomc_lr_unemp, lr_pce=fomc_lr_pce
)
display(HTML(html))
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""
Current economic conditions as of {now}:
- Real GDP growth: {gdp_latest:.1f}% (SAAR, latest quarter); prior quarter: {gdp_prev:.1f}%
- Unemployment rate: {unrate_latest:.1f}%; natural rate (CBO): {nrou_latest:.1f}%; gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp
- Core PCE inflation (YoY): {core_latest:.1f}%; Fed target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%
- Average monthly payroll gains (3-month avg): {payroll_3m:,.0f} thousand
- 10-year Treasury yield: {t10_latest:.2f}%; 10s-2s spread: {spread_10_2:+.2f} pp
- SPF projections: real GDP {spf_gdp_next}%; PCE inflation {spf_pce_next}% (next year medians)
- FOMC longer-run SEP: real GDP {fomc_lr_gdp}%; unemployment {fomc_lr_unemp}%; PCE {fomc_lr_pce}%

Write a 2-paragraph overview of current economic conditions suitable for the 
opening summary of a Tealbook A. The first paragraph covers real activity and 
the labor market. The second covers inflation and financial conditions.
"""

text = call_claude(TEALBOOK_SYSTEM, prompt, max_tokens=400)
print(text)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-overview
#| fig-cap: "Key indicators — overview"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 3.8))

# GDP
ax = axes[0]
g = gdp_g['2016-01-01':]
colors = [BLUE if v >= 0 else RED for v in g]
ax.bar(g.index, g.values, width=70, color=colors, alpha=0.85)
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.6)
ax.set_title('Real GDP Growth (%, SAAR)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.set_ylabel('Percent')
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Unemployment
ax = axes[1]
ur  = unrate['2016-01-01':]
nr  = nrou.reindex(ur.index, method='ffill')
ax.plot(ur.index, ur.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.8, label='Unemployment rate')
ax.plot(nr.index, nr.values, color=RED,  lw=1.3, ls='--', label='Natural rate (CBO)')
ax.fill_between(ur.index, ur.values, nr.values, alpha=0.12, color=RED)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Unemployment vs. Natural Rate (%)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Core PCE
ax = axes[2]
cp = core_pce['2016-01-01':]
ax.plot(cp.index, cp.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.8)
ax.axhline(2.0, color=RED, lw=0.9, ls=':', label='2% target')
ax.fill_between(cp.index, cp.values, 2.0, alpha=0.12,
                color=BLUE if cp.iloc[-1] >= 2 else RED)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Core PCE Inflation (YoY %)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

plt.tight_layout(pad=1.5)
plt.show()
```

---

## 📈 GDP & Output

```{python}
#| label: gdp-data
#| include: false

gdp_lvl  = pull_series('GDPC1',            start=START).dropna()
pot_gdp  = pull_series('GDPPOT',           start=START).dropna()
pce_real = pull_series('PCECC96',          start=START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
invest   = pull_series('GPDIC1',           start=START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
govt     = pull_series('GCEC1',            start=START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
netexp   = pull_series('NETEXC',           start=START).dropna()

# Output gap
og = ((gdp_lvl - pot_gdp.reindex(gdp_lvl.index, method='ffill')) /
       pot_gdp.reindex(gdp_lvl.index, method='ffill') * 100).dropna()

gdp_latest   = gdp_g.iloc[-1]
gdp_q_avg    = gdp_g.iloc[-4:].mean()
og_latest    = og.iloc[-1]
pce_latest   = pce_real.iloc[-1]
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""
GDP and output data as of {now}:
- Real GDP growth (latest quarter, SAAR): {gdp_latest:.1f}%
- Average real GDP growth over past 4 quarters: {gdp_q_avg:.1f}%
- Output gap (GDP minus potential, % of potential): {og_latest:.2f}%
- Real PCE growth (YoY): {pce_latest:.1f}%
- SPF median real GDP growth next 4 quarters: {spf_gdp_next}%
- FOMC longer-run real GDP growth: {fomc_lr_gdp}%

Write 2 paragraphs on real activity. First paragraph: GDP growth pace and 
composition (consumption, investment, government). Second paragraph: 
output gap and the growth outlook relative to potential and SPF/FOMC projections.
"""

text = call_claude(TEALBOOK_SYSTEM, prompt, max_tokens=400)
print(text)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-gdp
#| fig-cap: "Real GDP growth and output gap"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 3.8))

# GDP bar
ax = axes[0]
g = gdp_g['2016-01-01':]
colors = [BLUE if v >= 0 else RED for v in g]
ax.bar(g.index, g.values, width=70, color=colors, alpha=0.85)
ax.axhline(fomc_lr_gdp, color=GRAY, lw=0.9, ls=':',
           label=f'FOMC longer-run ({fomc_lr_gdp}%)')
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.6)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Real GDP Growth (%, SAAR)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.set_ylabel('Percent')
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Output gap
ax = axes[1]
gap = og['2016-01-01':]
ax.fill_between(gap.index, gap.values, 0,
                where=(gap.values >= 0), alpha=0.3, color=BLUE, label='Positive gap')
ax.fill_between(gap.index, gap.values, 0,
                where=(gap.values < 0), alpha=0.3, color=RED, label='Negative gap')
ax.plot(gap.index, gap.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.4)
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.6)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Output Gap (% of potential GDP)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Expenditure components
ax = axes[2]
for s, name, color, dash in [
    (pce_real,  'PCE',        BLUE,  'solid'),
    (invest,    'Investment',  RED,   'dashed'),
    (govt,      'Government',  GRAY,  'dotted'),
]:
    s2 = s['2016-01-01':]
    ax.plot(s2.index, s2.values, color=color, lw=1.6, ls=dash, label=name)
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.6)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Expenditure Components (YoY %)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

plt.tight_layout(pad=1.5)
plt.show()
```

---

## 👷 Labor Market

```{python}
#| label: labor-data
#| include: false

payrolls_lvl = pull_series('PAYEMS',       start=START).dropna()
payrolls     = payrolls_lvl.diff().dropna()
unrate       = pull_series('UNRATE',       start=START).dropna()
nrou         = pull_series('NROU',         start=START).dropna().resample('MS').interpolate()
lfpr         = pull_series('CIVPART',      start=START).dropna()
epop25       = pull_series('LNS12300060',  start=START).dropna()
u5           = pull_series('U5RATE',       start=START).dropna()
ahe          = pull_series('CES0500000003',start=START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
jolts_o      = pull_series('JTSJOR',       start=START).dropna()
jolts_q      = pull_series('JTSQUR',       start=START).dropna()
claims       = pull_series('IC4WSA',       start=START).dropna()

unrate_latest  = unrate.iloc[-1]
nrou_latest    = nrou.reindex(unrate.index, method='ffill').iloc[-1]
ugap           = unrate_latest - nrou_latest
payroll_3m     = payrolls.iloc[-3:].mean()
payroll_12m    = payrolls.iloc[-12:].mean()
lfpr_latest    = lfpr.iloc[-1]
ahe_latest     = ahe.iloc[-1]
claims_latest  = claims.iloc[-1]
```

```{python}
#| label: labor-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""
Labor market data as of {now}:
- Unemployment rate: {unrate_latest:.1f}%; natural rate (CBO): {nrou_latest:.1f}%; gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp
- Average monthly payroll gains: 3-month avg {payroll_3m:,.0f}K; 12-month avg {payroll_12m:,.0f}K
- Labor force participation rate: {lfpr_latest:.1f}%
- Average hourly earnings growth (YoY): {ahe_latest:.1f}%
- Initial unemployment claims (4-week MA): {claims_latest:,.0f}
- FOMC longer-run unemployment: {fomc_lr_unemp}%
- SPF median unemployment next year: approximately {fomc_lr_unemp + ugap*0.3:.1f}% (estimated from gap dynamics)

Write 2 paragraphs on the labor market. First paragraph: payroll job gains, 
unemployment gap, and tightness indicators. Second paragraph: wage growth, 
participation, and the outlook relative to FOMC longer-run estimates.
"""

text = call_claude(TEALBOOK_SYSTEM, prompt, max_tokens=400)
print(text)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-labor-1
#| fig-cap: "Payrolls, unemployment, and participation"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 3.8))

# Payrolls
ax = axes[0]
p = payrolls['2016-01-01':]
colors = [BLUE if v >= 0 else RED for v in p]
ax.bar(p.index, p.values, width=25, color=colors, alpha=0.85)
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.6)
ax.set_title('Change in Nonfarm Payrolls (thous., SA)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.set_ylabel('Thousands')
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Unemployment vs natural rate
ax = axes[1]
ur = unrate['2016-01-01':]
nr = nrou.reindex(ur.index, method='ffill')
ax.plot(ur.index, ur.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.8, label='U-3')
ax.plot(nr.index, nr.values, color=RED,  lw=1.3, ls='--', label='Natural rate')
ax.fill_between(ur.index, ur.values, nr.values, alpha=0.12, color=RED)
ax.axhline(fomc_lr_unemp, color=GRAY, lw=0.9, ls=':',
           label=f'FOMC longer-run ({fomc_lr_unemp}%)')
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Unemployment Rate (%)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# AHE + LFPR on twin axes
ax = axes[2]
ax2 = ax.twinx()
a = ahe['2016-01-01':]
l = lfpr['2016-01-01':]
ax.plot(a.index, a.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.8, label='AHE YoY %')
ax2.plot(l.index, l.values, color=ORANGE, lw=1.4, ls='--', label='LFPR (right)')
ax.set_ylabel('AHE YoY %', color=BLUE, fontsize=8)
ax2.set_ylabel('LFPR %', color=ORANGE, fontsize=8)
ax.set_title('Wage Growth & Participation', fontsize=9, pad=6)
lines1, labels1 = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
lines2, labels2 = ax2.get_legend_handles_labels()
ax.legend(lines1 + lines2, labels1 + labels2, fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

plt.tight_layout(pad=1.5)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-labor-2
#| fig-cap: "JOLTS and claims"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 3.8))

# JOLTS
ax = axes[0]
o = jolts_o['2016-01-01':].rolling(3).mean()
q = jolts_q['2016-01-01':].rolling(3).mean()
ax.plot(o.index, o.values, color=BLUE,  lw=1.8, label='Openings rate')
ax.plot(q.index, q.values, color=RED,   lw=1.6, ls='--', label='Quits rate')
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('JOLTS: Openings & Quits Rates (%, 3-mo MA)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Claims
ax = axes[1]
c = claims['2016-01-01':]
ax.plot(c.index, c.values, color=ORANGE, lw=1.8)
ax.set_title('Initial Unemployment Claims, 4-week MA (thous.)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.set_ylabel('Thousands')
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

plt.tight_layout(pad=1.5)
plt.show()
```

---

## 📉 Prices & Inflation

```{python}
#| label: inflation-data
#| include: false

cpi      = pull_series('CPIAUCSL',             start=START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
pce      = pull_series('PCEPI',                start=START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
core_pce = pull_series('PCEPILFE',             start=START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
trim_pce = pull_series('PCETRIM12M159SFRBDAL', start=START).dropna()
mkt_pce  = pull_series('DPCXRG3M086SBEA',     start=START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
eci      = pull_series('ECIWAG',               start=START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
tips_fwd = pull_series('T5YIFRM',             start=START).dropna()
clev5    = pull_series('EXPINF5YR',            start=START).dropna()

cpi_latest    = cpi.iloc[-1]
pce_latest    = pce.iloc[-1]
core_latest   = core_pce.iloc[-1]
trim_latest   = trim_pce.iloc[-1]
mkt_latest    = mkt_pce.iloc[-1]
tips_latest   = tips_fwd.iloc[-1]
eci_latest    = eci.iloc[-1]
```

```{python}
#| label: inflation-narrative
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""
Inflation data as of {now}:
- Headline PCE inflation (YoY): {pce_latest:.1f}%
- Core PCE inflation (ex. food & energy, YoY): {core_latest:.1f}%
- Trimmed mean PCE (12-month): {trim_latest:.1f}%
- Market-based core PCE (YoY): {mkt_latest:.1f}%
- Headline CPI (YoY): {cpi_latest:.1f}%
- Employment cost index, private wages (YoY): {eci_latest:.1f}%
- 5yr5yr forward TIPS inflation compensation: {tips_latest:.2f}%
- FOMC longer-run PCE inflation target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%
- SPF median PCE inflation next year: {spf_pce_next}%

Write 2 paragraphs on inflation. First paragraph: recent price developments 
across headline and core measures, noting any divergence. Second paragraph: 
labor cost pressures and inflation expectations relative to the FOMC target 
and SPF projections.
"""

text = call_claude(TEALBOOK_SYSTEM, prompt, max_tokens=400)
print(text)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-inflation
#| fig-cap: "Inflation measures"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 3.8))

# Headline
ax = axes[0]
for s, name, color, ls in [
    (cpi, 'CPI',  RED,  'solid'),
    (pce, 'PCE',  GRAY, 'solid'),
]:
    s2 = s['2016-01-01':]
    ax.plot(s2.index, s2.values, color=color, lw=1.8, ls=ls, label=name)
ax.axhline(2.0, color='black', lw=0.8, ls=':', label='2% target')
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.4)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Headline CPI & PCE (YoY %)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Core measures
ax = axes[1]
for s, name, color, ls in [
    (trim_pce, 'Trimmed mean',       BLUE,  'solid'),
    (mkt_pce,  'Market-based core',  RED,   'solid'),
    (core_pce, 'Core PCE (ex. F&E)', GRAY,  'solid'),
]:
    s2 = s['2016-01-01':]
    ax.plot(s2.index, s2.values, color=color, lw=1.6, ls=ls, label=name)
ax.axhline(2.0, color='black', lw=0.8, ls=':', label='2% target')
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Core PCE Measures (YoY %)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Expectations
ax = axes[2]
t = tips_fwd['2016-01-01':]
c = clev5['2016-01-01':]
ax.plot(t.index, t.values, color=RED,  lw=1.8, label='5yr5yr TIPS forward')
ax.plot(c.index, c.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.6, ls='--', label='Cleveland 5-yr expected')
ax.axhline(2.0, color='black', lw=0.8, ls=':', label='2% target')
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Inflation Expectations (%)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

plt.tight_layout(pad=1.5)
plt.show()
```

---

## 💰 Financial Conditions

```{python}
#| label: fin-data
#| include: false

dgs10  = pull_series('DGS10',        start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
dgs2   = pull_series('DGS2',         start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
dgs3m  = pull_series('DGS3MO',       start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
hy_spr = pull_series('BAMLH0A0HYM2', start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
ig_spr = pull_series('BAMLC0A4CBBB', start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
vix    = pull_series('VIXCLS',        start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
dollar = pull_series('DTWEXBGS',      start=START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
nfci   = pull_series('NFCI',         start=START).dropna()

spread_10_2 = (dgs10 - dgs2).dropna()
spread_10_3m = (dgs10 - dgs3m).dropna()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-financial
#| fig-cap: "Financial conditions"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 3.8))

# Yield curve
ax = axes[0]
for s, name, color, ls in [
    (dgs10,         '10-yr Treasury',   BLUE,  'solid'),
    (dgs2,          '2-yr Treasury',    RED,   'dashed'),
    (spread_10_2,   '10s–2s spread',    GRAY,  'dotted'),
]:
    s2 = s['2016-01-01':]
    ax.plot(s2.index, s2.values, color=color, lw=1.6, ls=ls, label=name)
ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.5)
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Treasury Yields & Spread (%)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# Credit spreads
ax = axes[1]
hy = hy_spr['2016-01-01':]
ig = ig_spr['2016-01-01':]
ax.plot(hy.index, hy.values, color=RED,  lw=1.8, label='HY spread (OAS)')
ax.plot(ig.index, ig.values, color=BLUE, lw=1.6, ls='--', label='BBB spread (OAS)')
ax.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('Credit Spreads (bp, OAS)', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

# VIX + NFCI
ax  = axes[2]
ax2 = ax.twinx()
v = vix['2016-01-01':]
n = nfci['2016-01-01':]
ax.plot(v.index, v.values,  color=ORANGE, lw=1.8, label='VIX')
ax2.plot(n.index, n.values, color=BLUE,   lw=1.4, ls='--', label='NFCI (right)')
ax2.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.5)
ax.set_ylabel('VIX', color=ORANGE, fontsize=8)
ax2.set_ylabel('NFCI', color=BLUE, fontsize=8)
lines1, l1 = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
lines2, l2 = ax2.get_legend_handles_labels()
ax.legend(lines1+lines2, l1+l2, fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
ax.set_title('VIX & Chicago Fed NFCI', fontsize=9, pad=6)
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%Y'))
ax.spines[['top','right']].set_visible(False)

plt.tight_layout(pad=1.5)
plt.show()
```

---

## 🔍 Special Topics

```{python}
#| label: special-topics-data
#| include: false

import urllib.request, json, re
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def fetch_fed_speeches(n: int = 5) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Scrape recent Federal Reserve speeches from the public RSS feed.
    Returns list of dicts with title, speaker, date, url.
    """
    url = "https://www.federalreserve.gov/feeds/speeches.xml"
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=10) as resp:
            content = resp.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
        items = re.findall(r'<item>(.*?)</item>', content, re.DOTALL)[:n]
        speeches = []
        for item in items:
            title   = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', item)
            date_s  = re.search(r'<dc:date>(.*?)</dc:date>', item)
            link    = re.search(r'<link>(.*?)</link>', item)
            speaker = re.search(r'<dc:creator>(.*?)</dc:creator>', item)
            speeches.append({
                'title':   title.group(1).strip()   if title   else 'Untitled',
                'date':    date_s.group(1)[:10]     if date_s  else '',
                'url':     link.group(1).strip()    if link    else '#',
                'speaker': speaker.group(1).strip() if speaker else 'Federal Reserve',
            })
        return speeches
    except Exception:
        return []


def fetch_feds_papers(n: int = 4) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Fetch recent FEDS working papers via Federal Reserve RSS feed.
    """
    url = "https://www.federalreserve.gov/feeds/feds.xml"
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=10) as resp:
            content = resp.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
        items = re.findall(r'<item>(.*?)</item>', content, re.DOTALL)[:n]
        papers = []
        for item in items:
            title  = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', item)
            date_s = re.search(r'<dc:date>(.*?)</dc:date>', item)
            link   = re.search(r'<link>(.*?)</link>', item)
            desc   = re.search(r'<description>(.*?)</description>', item, re.DOTALL)
            papers.append({
                'title': title.group(1).strip()        if title  else 'Untitled',
                'date':  date_s.group(1)[:10]          if date_s else '',
                'url':   link.group(1).strip()         if link   else '#',
                'desc':  (re.sub(r'<.*?>','',desc.group(1)).strip()[:180]+'…')
                          if desc else '',
            })
        return papers
    except Exception:
        return []

speeches = fetch_fed_speeches(6)
papers   = fetch_feds_papers(4)
```

```{python}
#| label: special-topics-llm
#| output: asis

# Build context for LLM from live-fetched speeches + papers
speech_lines = "\n".join(
    f"- [{s['date']}] {s['speaker']}: \"{s['title']}\""
    for s in speeches
) if speeches else "- [No speeches retrieved]"

paper_lines = "\n".join(
    f"- [{p['date']}] \"{p['title']}\""
    for p in papers
) if papers else "- [No papers retrieved]"

st_prompt = f"""
You are identifying potential Tealbook special topic candidates for an upcoming FOMC meeting.
Today's date: {now}

Recent Federal Reserve speeches:
{speech_lines}

Recent FEDS working papers:
{paper_lines}

Current conditions context:
- Core PCE: {core_latest:.1f}% (target: 2.0%)
- Unemployment gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp vs. natural rate
- 10s-2s yield spread: {spread_10_2.iloc[-1]:+.2f} pp

Based on the speeches, working papers, and current economic conditions, 
propose 3 potential Tealbook special topics. For each, give:
1. A concise title (10 words or fewer)
2. One sentence explaining why it is policy-relevant right now
3. One sentence on what data or analysis would anchor the discussion

Format as three clearly numbered items. Be specific, not generic.
"""

st_text = call_claude(TEALBOOK_SYSTEM, st_prompt, max_tokens=500)
print("### Suggested Special Topics for This FOMC Cycle\n")
print(st_text)
print()
```

```{python}
#| label: special-topics-display
from IPython.display import display, HTML

# Speeches table
if speeches:
    rows_html = ""
    for i, s in enumerate(speeches):
        bg = "#f5f7fb" if i % 2 == 0 else "white"
        rows_html += f"""
        <tr style="background:{bg};">
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;color:#555;">{s['date']}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;">{s['speaker']}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;">
            <a href="{s['url']}" target="_blank"
               style="color:#003087;text-decoration:none;">{s['title']}</a>
          </td>
        </tr>"""
    speeches_html = f"""
    <h4 style="margin-top:1.4rem;color:#003087;">Recent Fed Speeches</h4>
    <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.88rem;">
      <thead>
        <tr style="background:#003087;color:white;">
          <th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:left;width:10%;">Date</th>
          <th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:left;width:22%;">Speaker</th>
          <th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:left;">Title</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>{rows_html}</tbody>
    </table>"""
else:
    speeches_html = "<p><em>Fed speeches feed unavailable.</em></p>"

# Papers table
if papers:
    rows_html = ""
    for i, p in enumerate(papers):
        bg = "#f5f7fb" if i % 2 == 0 else "white"
        rows_html += f"""
        <tr style="background:{bg};">
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;color:#555;">{p['date']}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 10px;">
            <a href="{p['url']}" target="_blank"
               style="color:#003087;text-decoration:none;">{p['title']}</a>
            <br><span style="font-size:0.80rem;color:#666;">{p['desc']}</span>
          </td>
        </tr>"""
    papers_html = f"""
    <h4 style="margin-top:1.6rem;color:#003087;">Recent FEDS Working Papers</h4>
    <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.88rem;">
      <thead>
        <tr style="background:#003087;color:white;">
          <th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:left;width:10%;">Date</th>
          <th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:left;">Title & Abstract</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>{rows_html}</tbody>
    </table>"""
else:
    papers_html = "<p><em>FEDS papers feed unavailable.</em></p>"

display(HTML(speeches_html + papers_html))
```

:::

---

*Shadow Tealbook A — Purdue University. Data: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis), BLS, BEA. LLM narrative: Anthropic Claude. This document is a research prototype and does not represent the views of the Federal Reserve System.*
